content:
type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Editing NDCG.compute() to fix IDCG calculation to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))

fields.note: Editing NDCG.compute() to fix IDCG calculation to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))

content:
type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: In NDCG.compute(), fix the IDCG (ideal DCG) calculation. Currently it computes ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) and sums discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, ideal_length+1). This is WRONG: IDCG must be computed based on the number of expected relevant documents only, not clamped by the number of retrieved documents. Change it to: idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)) — i.e. remove the min()/ideal_length clamping against len(retrieved_ids) entirely and iterate directly over range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1). This should compose fine with your relevance_scores parameter addition — just make sure the corrected iteration bound (len(expected_ids), not min(retrieved,expected)) is preserved when you thread relevance_scores through the idcg sum. Everything else in compute() (the dcg sum, the expected_set docid membership check, edge-case idcg==0 handling) can stay as-is structurally aside from your relevance_scores change.
note: IDCG must reflect ideal ranking of ALL expected relevant docs, not be capped by retrieved_ids length

Currently it computes ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) and sums discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, ideal_length+1). This is WRONG: IDCG must be computed based on the number of expected relevant documents only, not clamped by the number of retrieved documents. Change it to: idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)) — i.e. remove the min()/ideal_length clamping against len(retrieved_ids) entirely and iterate directly over range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1). This should compose fine with your relevance_scores parameter addition — just make sure the corrected iteration bound (len(expected_ids), not min(retrieved,expected)) is preserved when you thread relevance_scores through the idcg sum. Everything else in compute() (the dcg sum, the expected_set docid membership check, edge-case idcg==0 handling) can stay as-is structurally aside from your relevance_scores change.
fields.note: IDCG must reflect ideal ranking of ALL expected relevant docs, not be capped by retrieved_ids length

content:
type: SPEC
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: In NDCG.compute(), the IDCG calculation must iterate over the number of EXPECTED relevant documents, not min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). Replace:
    ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))
    idcg = sum(
        discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode)
        for i in range(1, ideal_length + 1)
    )
with:
    idcg = sum(
        discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode)
        for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)
    )
Rationale: standard NDCG defines IDCG as the DCG of the ideal ranking of all relevant (expected) documents, regardless of how many docs were retrieved. Clamping to len(retrieved_ids) under-counts IDCG when fewer docs are retrieved than are truly relevant, inflating the NDCG score. Please preserve this iteration bound (len(expected_ids)) when integrating your relevance_scores parameter into the idcg sum.
note: Exact replacement for the ideal_length/idcg block in NDCG.compute()

fields.note: Exact replacement for the ideal_length/idcg block in NDCG.compute()

content:
type: DONE
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Applied the idcg fix (range(1, len(expected_ids)+1)) in my own copy since our sandboxes aren't filesystem-shared; noted your relevance_scores feature is separate and out of scope for my patch. Confirmed via test run that test_ndcg Case 4 now correctly fails (encodes old buggy behavior) as expected.
